---
title: "Mesfin Genie"
pagetitle: "Mesfin Genie | Health Economist"
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
description: "Mesfin Genie is a Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at the University of Newcastle, specialising in health preferences, choice modelling and public health policy."
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero" aria-labelledby="hero-title"><div class="page-shell hero-grid">
<div class="portrait-frame reveal"><img src="/assets/mesfin-genie-profile-square.webp" alt="Portrait of Mesfin Genie" width="640" height="640" fetchpriority="high"></div>
<div class="hero-copy reveal"><h1 id="hero-title">Mesfin Genie</h1><p class="hero-role">Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</p><p class="hero-affiliation">Newcastle Business School, The University of Newcastle</p><p class="hero-value">I study how patients and the public value healthcare, process information and respond to policy, using choice modelling, behavioural experiments and applied econometrics.</p><div class="hero-actions"><a class="button-primary" href="/publications/">Publications</a><a class="button-secondary" href="/cv/">Curriculum vitae</a></div><div class="social-row" aria-label="Profiles"><a class="social-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" aria-label="Google Scholar" title="Google Scholar" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5.242 13.769L0 9.5 12 0l12 9.5-5.242 4.269C17.548 11.249 14.978 9.5 12 9.5c-2.977 0-5.548 1.748-6.758 4.269zM12 10a7 7 0 1 0 0 14 7 7 0 0 0 0-14z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" aria-label="ORCID" title="ORCID" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M12 0C5.372 0 0 5.372 0 12s5.372 12 12 12 12-5.372 12-12S18.628 0 12 0zM7.369 4.378c.525 0 .947.431.947.947s-.422.947-.947.947a.95.95 0 0 1-.947-.947c0-.525.422-.947.947-.947zm-.722 3.038h1.444v10.041H6.647V7.416zm3.562 0h3.9c3.712 0 5.344 2.653 5.344 5.025 0 2.578-2.016 5.025-5.325 5.025h-3.919V7.416zm1.444 1.303v7.444h2.297c3.272 0 4.022-2.484 4.022-3.722 0-2.016-1.284-3.722-4.097-3.722h-2.222z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mesfin-Genie" aria-label="ResearchGate" title="ResearchGate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M19.586 0c-.818 0-1.508.19-2.073.565-.563.377-.97.936-1.213 1.68a3.193 3.193 0 0 0-.112.437 8.365 8.365 0 0 0-.078.53 9 9 0 0 0-.05.727c-.01.282-.013.621-.013 1.016a31.121 31.123 0 0 0 .014 1.017 9 9 0 0 0 .05.727 7.946 7.946 0 0 0 .077.53h-.005a3.334 3.334 0 0 0 .113.438c.245.743.65 1.303 1.214 1.68.565.376 1.256.564 2.075.564.8 0 1.536-.213 2.105-.603.57-.39.94-.916 1.175-1.65.076-.235.135-.558.177-.93a10.9 10.9 0 0 0 .043-1.207v-.82c0-.095-.047-.142-.14-.142h-3.064c-.094 0-.14.047-.14.141v.956c0 .094.046.14.14.14h1.666c.056 0 .084.03.084.086 0 .36 0 .62-.036.865-.038.244-.1.447-.147.606-.108.385-.348.664-.638.876-.29.212-.738.35-1.227.35-.545 0-.901-.15-1.21-.353-.306-.203-.517-.454-.67-.915a3.136 3.136 0 0 1-.147-.762 17.366 17.367 0 0 1-.034-.656c-.01-.26-.014-.572-.014-.939a26.401 26.403 0 0 1 .014-.938 15.821 15.822 0 0 1 .035-.656 3.19 3.19 0 0 1 .148-.76 1.89 1.89 0 0 1 .742-1.01c.344-.244.593-.352 1.137-.352.508 0 .815.096 1.144.303.33.207.528.492.764.925.047.094.111.118.198.07l1.044-.43c.075-.048.09-.115.042-.199a3.549 3.549 0 0 0-.466-.742 3 3 0 0 0-.679-.607 3.313 3.313 0 0 0-.903-.41A4.068 4.068 0 0 0 19.586 0zM8.217 5.836c-1.69 0-3.036.086-4.297.086-1.146 0-2.291 0-3.007-.029v.831l1.088.2c.744.144 1.174.488 1.174 2.264v11.288c0 1.777-.43 2.12-1.174 2.263l-1.088.2v.832c.773-.029 2.12-.086 3.465-.086 1.29 0 2.951.057 3.667.086v-.831l-1.49-.2c-.773-.115-1.174-.487-1.174-2.264v-4.784c.688.057 1.29.057 2.206.057 1.748 3.123 3.41 5.472 4.355 6.56.86 1.032 2.177 1.691 3.839 1.691.487 0 1.003-.086 1.318-.23v-.744c-1.031 0-2.063-.716-2.808-1.518-1.26-1.376-2.95-3.582-4.355-6.074 2.32-.545 4.04-2.722 4.04-4.9 0-3.208-2.492-4.698-5.758-4.698zm-.515 1.29c2.406 0 3.839 1.26 3.839 3.552 0 2.263-1.547 3.782-4.097 3.782-.974 0-1.404-.03-2.063-.086v-7.19c.66-.059 1.547-.059 2.32-.059z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://au.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie-ph-d-65634216" aria-label="LinkedIn" title="LinkedIn" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M20.447 20.452h-3.554v-5.569c0-1.328-.027-3.037-1.852-3.037-1.853 0-2.136 1.445-2.136 2.939v5.667H9.351V9h3.414v1.561h.046c.477-.9 1.637-1.85 3.37-1.85 3.601 0 4.267 2.37 4.267 5.455v6.286zM5.337 7.433a2.062 2.062 0 0 1-2.063-2.065 2.064 2.064 0 1 1 2.063 2.065zm1.782 13.019H3.555V9h3.564v11.452zM22.225 0H1.771C.792 0 0 .774 0 1.729v20.542C0 23.227.792 24 1.771 24h20.451C23.2 24 24 23.227 24 22.271V1.729C24 .774 23.2 0 22.222 0h.003z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://github.com/DrGenie" aria-label="GitHub" title="GitHub" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M12 .297c-6.63 0-12 5.373-12 12 0 5.303 3.438 9.8 8.205 11.385.6.113.82-.258.82-.577 0-.285-.01-1.04-.015-2.04-3.338.724-4.042-1.61-4.042-1.61C4.422 18.07 3.633 17.7 3.633 17.7c-1.087-.744.084-.729.084-.729 1.205.084 1.838 1.236 1.838 1.236 1.07 1.835 2.809 1.305 3.495.998.108-.776.417-1.305.76-1.605-2.665-.3-5.466-1.332-5.466-5.93 0-1.31.465-2.38 1.235-3.22-.135-.303-.54-1.523.105-3.176 0 0 1.005-.322 3.3 1.23.96-.267 1.98-.399 3-.405 1.02.006 2.04.138 3 .405 2.28-1.552 3.285-1.23 3.285-1.23.645 1.653.24 2.873.12 3.176.765.84 1.23 1.91 1.23 3.22 0 4.61-2.805 5.625-5.475 5.92.42.36.81 1.096.81 2.22 0 1.606-.015 2.896-.015 3.286 0 .315.21.69.825.57C20.565 22.092 24 17.592 24 12.297c0-6.627-5.373-12-12-12"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://x.com/mesfin_genie" aria-label="X" title="X" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M14.234 10.162 22.977 0h-2.072l-7.591 8.824L7.251 0H.258l9.168 13.343L.258 24H2.33l8.016-9.318L16.749 24h6.993zm-2.837 3.299-.929-1.329L3.076 1.56h3.182l5.965 8.532.929 1.329 7.754 11.09h-3.182z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" aria-label="University profile" title="University profile" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 10.5 12 5l9 5.5v1.7H3zM5 14h2v5H5zm4 0h2v5H9zm4 0h2v5h-2zm4 0h2v5h-2zM3 21h18v2H3z"/></svg></a><a class="social-link" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au" aria-label="Email" title="Email"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 5h18v14H3zm2 2v.4l7 5.1 7-5.1V7zm14 10V9.8l-7 5.1-7-5.1V17z"/></svg></a></div></div>
</div></section>
<section class="credibility-band" aria-label="Research profile"><div class="page-shell credibility-grid"><a class="credibility-item" data-metric-tile="citations" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" hidden><strong data-metric="citations"></strong><span>Citations, Google Scholar</span></a><a class="credibility-item" data-metric-tile="hindex" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" hidden><strong data-metric="hindex"></strong><span>h-index, Google Scholar</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="/publications/"><strong>42</strong><span>Peer-reviewed and other outputs</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="https://www.grants.gov.au/Ga/Show/57849742-1674-4b10-b75a-47d575e5d430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><strong>A$4.75M</strong><span>Programme funding secured</span></a><a class="credibility-item" href="/cv/"><strong>4</strong><span>Countries of appointment</span></a></div></section>
<section class="section" aria-labelledby="research-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Research</p><h2 id="research-heading">Health decisions, preferences and policy</h2></div><a href="/research/">Research overview →</a></div><div class="pillar-grid"><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>Preferences</span><h3>Health preferences and choice modelling</h3><p>Patient and public preferences, discrete choice experiments, preference heterogeneity, equity and value assessment.</p></article><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>Behaviour</span><h3>Decision processes</h3><p>Attention, information processing, numeracy, feedback, attribute ordering and eye-tracking evidence.</p></article><article class="pillar-card reveal"><span>Policy</span><h3>Public health policy</h3><p>Vaccination, mandates, telehealth, preventive care and economic evaluation of health programmes.</p></article></div></div></section>
<section class="section section-muted" aria-labelledby="recent-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Selected work</p><h2 id="recent-heading">Recent publications</h2></div><a href="/publications/">All publications →</a></div><div class="publication-teaser-grid"><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">Health Economics · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments</a></h3><p><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Shelby D. Reed; Semra Ozdemir</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">Social Science &amp; Medicine · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Fit for Purpose? Assessing the Robustness of Discrete Choice Experiment Designs in the Context of Evolving Health Technologies</a></h3><p><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Verity Watson; Michaël Schwarzinger; Stéphane Luchini</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article><article class="publication-teaser reveal"><div class="publication-kicker">Health Policy · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Feeling Lonely? Preferences for Support Programmes to Reduce Loneliness among Older Adults in Australia: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Allison W. Boyes; Francesco Paolucci</p><div class="publication-foot"><span class="status-tag">Published</span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></div></article></div></div></section>
<section class="section" aria-labelledby="tools-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Tools</p><h2 id="tools-heading">Decision support and economic evaluation</h2></div><a href="/tools/">All tools →</a></div><div class="tool-page-grid"><article class="tool-page-card"><div class="tool-meta">Vaccine policy · Live</div><h3>eMANDEVAL Future</h3><p>Prospective vaccine-mandate policy analysis using predicted public support, expected lives saved and benefit-cost outcomes.</p><a href="/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch tool ↗</a></article><article class="tool-page-card"><div class="tool-meta">Farm economics · Live</div><h3>SOIL CRC Benefit-Cost Analysis Tool</h3><p>Farm-practice evaluation using net present value, benefit-cost ratio, return on investment and sensitivity analysis.</p><a href="/farming-bca-tool-v18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch tool ↗</a></article><article class="tool-page-card"><div class="tool-meta">Field epidemiology · Live</div><h3>STEPS Decision Aid</h3><p>Decision-support tool developed for the World Bank Field Epidemiology Training Programme (FETP), supporting prioritisation and planning under budget constraints.</p><a href="/STEPS-FETP-DecisionAid-V20/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch tool ↗</a></article></div></div></section>
<section class="section section-muted" aria-labelledby="webinar-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Talks and media</p><h2 id="webinar-heading">Featured webinar</h2></div><a href="/talks/">Talks and media →</a></div><div class="video-embed"><div class="video-frame" data-embed="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/HO2qXJp-BFs?rel=0"><button class="video-play" type="button" aria-label="Play the featured webinar (loads the YouTube player)"><span class="video-play-icon" aria-hidden="true">&#9658;</span><span>Play webinar</span></button></div></div><p class="video-note">A recorded webinar featuring Dr Mesfin Genie. The player loads only when you choose to play it. <a href="https://youtu.be/HO2qXJp-BFs" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Watch on YouTube</a>.</p></div></section><section class="section" aria-labelledby="teach-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Teaching and supervision</p><h2 id="teach-heading">Teaching and research students</h2></div><a href="/teaching/">Teaching and supervision →</a></div><p class="section-lead">I coordinate ECON3111 Behavioural Economics, ECON2112 Health Economics and Finance, and ECON3011 Introduction to Health Economics and Finance at the University of Newcastle, and share open <a href="/teaching/behavioural-economics/">behavioural economics resources</a>. I welcome enquiries from prospective PhD and MPhil students; see <a href="/supervision/">research supervision</a> for what to include.</p></div></section><section class="section section-muted" aria-labelledby="writing-heading"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Latest writing</p><h2 id="writing-heading">From the blog</h2></div><a href="/blog/">All posts →</a></div><div class="publication-teaser-grid"><article class="publication-teaser"><div class="publication-kicker">Choice modelling explained</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/why-99-does-not-feel-like-100/">Why 99% Does Not Feel Like 100%</a></h3><p>The certainty effect, and why a guaranteed outcome feels special.</p></article><article class="publication-teaser"><div class="publication-kicker">Everyday behavioural economics</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/insurance-and-lottery-tickets/">Why People Buy Both Insurance and Lottery Tickets</a></h3><p>How probability weighting makes both attractive at once.</p></article><article class="publication-teaser"><div class="publication-kicker">Choice modelling explained</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/risk-versus-ambiguity/">Risk versus Ambiguity</a></h3><p>Why unknown probabilities change decisions, especially for new health technologies.</p></article></div></div></section><section class="contact-strip"><div class="page-shell contact-grid"><div><p class="eyebrow">Contact</p><h2>Research, collaboration and supervision</h2><p>mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au</p></div><a class="button-primary" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au">Email Mesfin</a> <a class="button-secondary" href="/contact/">Contact page</a></div></section>
```
